topic: Using arrays to programmatically manage and animate multiple objects, and implementing visual depth using parallax principles.

concepts.name: arrays of objects
concepts.explanation: Instead of creating individual variables like ufo1, ufo2, etc., you create an empty array `[]` and use `.push()` inside a loop to add objects with randomized properties into it.
concepts.why_it_matters: It allows for scalability; you can change one number to go from 3 objects to 100 objects without writing more lines of code.

concepts.name: for...in loop for arrays
concepts.explanation: A loop used to iterate through an array where the variable (e.g., `el`) represents the index of the current element being processed.
concepts.why_it_matters: It provides a concise way to perform the same action (like drawing or moving) on every single item in a collection.

concepts.name: parallax scrolling
concepts.explanation: A technique where objects move at different speeds and have different scales to create an illusion of depth/distance.
concepts.why_it_matters: It adds a sense of 3D space to a 2D environment by linking an object's speed property to its visual scale.

concepts.name: mapping speed to scale
concepts.explanation: Using the object's speed property (`obj.s`) divided by a constant to determine its `scale()` in the draw function.
concepts.why_it_matters: It automates the visual depth effect so that faster-moving objects automatically appear larger (closer) and slower ones appear smaller (further away).

code_examples.demonstrates: Populating an array with 20 unique objects containing randomized position and speed data.
code_examples.common_issue: Forgetting to define the properties inside an object literal before pushing it, or using a loop that doesn't match the desired count.

code_examples.demonstrates: Iterating through an array of objects to call functions on each specific object using its index.
code_examples.common_issue: Passing the index `el` directly to a function that expects an object, rather than passing `ufoArr[el]`.

code_examples.demonstrates: Using a property of the object to dynamically change its visual size.
code_examples.common_issue: Dividing by a number that is too small, making the objects explode in size and cover the whole screen.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: The loop isn't working or the array is empty.
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Trying to use a `for...in` loop on an array that hasn't been populated yet (the length is still zero).
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Use a standard `for` loop to populate the array first, then use loops to iterate over it.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: The depth looks wrong; small objects are appearing on top of large ones.
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: The array is being drawn in the order it was created, rather than by size/speed.
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Sort the array so that objects intended to be in the background are drawn first.

key_moments.description: The instructor shows the difference between manual object creation and using an array with `.push()`.
Using `console.log(ufoArr)` to inspect the data structure and confirm that 20 objects were successfully created.
The instructor uses a Wikipedia article to explain the real-world concept of Parallax Scrolling, providing context for why we link speed and scale.

student_questions: How do I make 100 things happen instead of just 3?
Why are all my shapes in one spot? (referring to not using random values)
How do I loop through a list of objects?
My circles are all on top of each other in the wrong order
How do I make things look like they have depth?
What is the difference between ufo1 and ufoArr[i]?
How do I make objects move at different speeds automatically?
Why is my console showing 'Object' instead of the actual numbers?
How do I use a loop to draw many things at once?
